meaning: adj.有感觉的,明智的,有判断力的
examples: A luggage rack is a sensible option.
It might be sensible to get a lawyer.
The former option would be much more sensible.
Innovation flourishes in a benign environment of low taxes, a sensible judiciary and commonsense regulation.
Expertly cited numerous research studies, sensible conclusions (though radical to our culture)balanced and fair reporting.
